summary: Reject the D4.2 plan of reaching <700 dispatches/token through batched D1.1-D1.6 fusions. M.4 reports 877 dispatches/token, so the plan needs to remove at least 178 dispatches/token. D1.2/D1.3/D1.6 found no dataflow-safe material launch reduction, and the remaining D1 opt-in routes were either already defaulted, launch-count-neutral, or regressed under graph replay.

d1_decision_ledger.dispatch_reduction_if_counted: about 30/tok for linear-attention rotate2; full-attention Q/K path remains launch-count neutral because V still rotates separately
d1_decision_ledger.result: rejected; opt-in rotate-staged path regressed 512/128 graph decode 115.450 -> 110.457 tok/s (-4.32%)
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: not usable for D4.2; barrier/global-staging cost outweighed removed dispatches

d1_decision_ledger.result: deferred/no-op; input RMSNorm and add-RMSNorm are multi-consumer fanout, final norm -> lm-head is only ~0.04% kernel-time upper bound
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: no material single-use producer while preserving pack8/W8A16 layout

d1_decision_ledger.result: rejected/no-op; material same-input pairs are already fused, and full-attention Q/K/V widening was parent-rejected
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: no remaining standalone same-input pair with arithmetic/data-reuse upside

d1_decision_ledger.dispatch_reduction_if_counted: 0 for safe path; a direct selected-down+combine could remove 40/tok but is rejected
d1_decision_ledger.result: existing weighted_sum_shared_gate_combine_residual path is already the safe fused default; direct down+combine parent microbench was 13.38 -> 16.52 us (0.81x)
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: further launch removal collapses selected-expert grid parallelism

d1_decision_ledger.dispatch_reduction_if_counted: 0 in current implementation because logits+select becomes counter memset+cooperative kernel; an ideal graph-internal counter reset would at most save ~40/tok
d1_decision_ledger.result: rejected; graph replay decode regressed 512/128 by -0.93% and 4K/128 by -0.67%
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: producer occupancy can be preserved, but atomic/memset tail erases the win

d1_decision_ledger.result: rejected; opt-in Q+K/V pairing is correct but launch-count neutral and 4K decode regressed -0.21%
d1_decision_ledger.dataflow_verdict: changes pairing, not dispatch count; no robust decode win

include_rejected_countable_d1_1_plus_hyp.note: This already counts rejected/regressing paths; it still lands around 807 dispatches/token.

include_direct_selected_combine_too.note: This includes the parent-rejected selected-down+combine shape and still lands around 767 dispatches/token.

crosses_700_only_with_multi_consumer_nor.note: The remaining reduction would require fusing multi-consumer RMSNorm/rotation/W4/MoE families or multi-layer/megakernel scheduling, which is outside D1.1-D1.6 and violates the current real-dataflow evidence.

rationale: M.4 baseline is 877 dispatches/token; <700 requires removing at least 178 dispatches/token.
D1.2, D1.3, and D1.6 provide zero dataflow-safe launch-count reduction.

The only countable D1 launch removals either regressed (D1.1), were already safely fused with no further count reduction (D1.4), or are launch-count neutral/regressing due to required counter/reset work (D1.5).
Even an optimistic accounting of rejected D1.1 + ideal router + rejected direct combine removes only about 110 dispatches/token, leaving ~767 dispatches/token.
Therefore D4.2 as a Vulkan-style batched D1.1-D1.6 fusion plan fails the below-700 dispatch target and the real data-flow benefit criterion.
decision.future_reopen_condition: Reopen only as a new design that changes major data flow (multi-layer W4/MoE/attention megakernel, row-staged multi-consumer norm with proven no barrier regression, or scheduler-level graph compaction) and re-profiles dispatches/token before implementation.

commands: python3 - <<'PY'  # assert dispatch-cap math and no default change
python3 - <<'PY'  # assert docs/README/CHANGELOG reference the artifact
validation.result: passed; artifact JSON parsed, dispatch-cap math assertions passed, and docs/benchmark rollup references were present
